data_in.description: Feature list containing m/z values (CSV or text format)

data_out.description: Annotated feature table with Kendrick mass defect values and homologous series group assignments

domain_knowledge: Kendrick mass defect is calculated by converting exact m/z to the Kendrick scale (typically using CH₂ = 14.0157 Da as the reference unit) and computing the difference between the nominal and exact Kendrick mass; features with identical KMD values represent homologous series differing by integer multiples of CH₂ units.
PFAS compounds often form homologous series with repeating CH₂ units (chain extensions), making KMD analysis a key signature for identifying potential PFAS candidates in non-target screening.

The choice of reference unit for Kendrick scaling (e.g., CH₂ vs. CF₂) affects the KMD pattern; for PFAS screening, CH₂-based Kendrick mass is commonly used to detect hydrocarbon-chain-extension homologs.

KMD grouping tolerance (the allowed deviation from exact KMD equality when clustering features into series) typically ranges from 0.001 to 0.005 Da and depends on instrument mass accuracy and resolving power.

direct_checks: verify file exists in github:JonZwe__PFAScreen repository containing KMD calculation implementation
verify KMD module accepts a feature list as input (file or data structure with m/z and intensity fields)
verify KMD module returns grouped or flagged features with at least one output field indicating homologous series membership or KMD cluster assignment
script_runs: KMD filter executes without error on a test feature list with ≥10 features spanning m/z 200–1200
verify output contains KMD values (numeric, robust to rounding within ±0.01 Da)
verify features are grouped or flagged by KMD values with homologous series labels or cluster identifiers
expert_review: assess whether KMD calculation follows established mass defect formulae for PFAS homolog detection (expert judgment on correctness of Kendrick mass basis and defect computation)
evaluate whether grouping/flagging threshold for homologous series is chemically defensible and documented
review whether output interpretation and labeling of PFAS series are consistent with literature conventions for KMD-based PFAS screening

task_description: Implement a standalone Kendrick mass defect (KMD) analysis module that accepts a feature list (m/z values) and returns features grouped or flagged by their KMD values to identify homologous PFAS series.
task_objective: Implement a KMD analysis filter that accepts a feature list and returns features flagged or grouped by Kendrick mass defect values to identify homologous PFAS series.

task_outputs.label: Annotated feature table with KMD values and series assignments

title: Reconstruct the Kendrick Mass Defect (KMD) analysis filter for PFAS feature prioritization
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Parse input feature list containing m/z values. 2. Calculate Kendrick mass for each feature using the formula: Kendrick mass = (nominal mass / exact mass) × exact mass, where exact mass is converted to the Kendrick scale (e.g., CH₂ = 14.0157 Da). 3. Compute Kendrick mass defect (KMD) as the difference between nominal Kendrick mass and exact Kendrick mass. 4. Group or flag features with equivalent or near-equivalent KMD values (within user-specified tolerance) to identify homologous series members. 5. Output annotated feature table with KMD values and series group assignments.
